rulings.Case Title: J.P. MORGAN SECURITIES LLC VS. JEROME HUDDLESTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner J.P. Morgan Securities Llc?S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2025 Line 1. PETITIONER J.P. MORGAN SECURITIES LLC Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner J.P. Morgan Securities Llc?S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment. Petitioner J.P. Morgan Securities LLC's unopposed Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the Petition to Confirm the Arbitrators' Award is granted and the arbitration is confirmed in its entirety and Judgment shall be entered consistent with the Award, including an order to expunge from Byrne's CRD records all references to Occurrence Number 2264504. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SUN YOUNG HWANG ET AL VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Attendance At Depositions Of The Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sunrise'S Person(S) Most Qualified, Set Two And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Supporting Delcaration Of John W. Ranucci
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2025 Line 8. 2 - DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION's Motion For Order Compelling Attendance At Depositions Of The Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sunrise'S Person(S) Most Qualified, Set Two And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.00. The Hertz Corporation's motion to compel attendance of Sunrise's person most qualified (set two) is GRANTED. The PMQ/documents notice at issue is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP section 2017.010.) Sunrise (sued as Big O Tires) failed to produce a PMQ or produce documents for all listed categories. Sunrise has not demonstrated the merit of any objection. Hertz engaged in adequate meet and confer, including demonstrating that it contacted Sunrise to inquire about the nonappearance. (See CCP section 2025.450(b)(1).) The Ranucci declaration demonstrates that Sunrise has unreasonably failed to produce the person most qualified and the related documents for the categories at issue. It must do so forthwith, but no later than March 14, 2025. The court finds Sunrise did not act with substantial justification. Sunrise shall pay $2,250 as sanctions to Hertz Corporation forthwith but in no event later than March 14, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)
